data.title: HOYTS Cinemas and IMAX Expand Partnership with Ten New IMAX with Laser Locations Across Australia and New Zealand
data.description: In New York, IMAX Corporation (NYSE: IMAX) and HOYTS Cinemas, Australia’s leading cinema chain with over 30% of national admissions, have unveiled a deal for the establishment of ten advanced IMAX® with Laser theaters in Australia and New Zealand. This agreement marks a notable expansion of their current collaboration, which saw four IMAX cinemas added last year, almost doubling IMAX's presence in Australia. This move will significantly enhance IMAX's footprint with HOYTS.

data.title: Ford Likely To Report Higher Q1 Earnings; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: Ford Motor Company (NYSE: F) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings on April 29th after the market closes. Analysts are predicting earnings of 19 cents per share, an increase from 14 cents per share reported in the same period last year. The consensus for Ford's quarterly revenue stands at $38.93 billion, up from $37.42 billion in the previous year. Ford has surpassed revenue expectations for the past four quarters and seven out of the last 10 quarters. The company's stock closed at $12.40 on Tuesday, a 0.7% decrease. For more details and the most up-to-date analyst ratings, readers can visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page, where information can be sorted by various categories. Check out Benzinga.com for the full story.

data.title: What's Going On With Lithia Motors Stock Wednesday?
data.description: Lithia Motors Inc. (NYSE: LAD) released its first-quarter 2026 financial results, exceeding analyst projections for both earnings and revenue despite a drop in adjusted profits compared to the previous year. The auto retailer, headquartered in Medford, Oregon, reported adjusted diluted earnings of $7.34 per share, surpassing the consensus estimate of $6.83 but down from $7.93 per share in the same period last year. Quarterly sales totaled $9.271 billion, slightly beating the anticipated $9.222 billion and showing a slight increase from the $9.178 billion in the previous year's quarter. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga Pro.

data.title: Bitcoin touted as top inflation hedge by Paul Tudor Jones. Crypto prices rise pre-FOMC despite oil surge; Polymarket plans U.S. expansion.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies are experiencing a resurgence in value as they head into the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, despite a surge in oil prices. At the same time, Polymarket is actively pursuing opportunities to expand its presence in the United States. This development comes amidst growing interest in the crypto market from investors and traders. The rebound in crypto majors has seen Bitcoin rise by 5%, Ethereum by 3%, and other major altcoins following suit. Oil prices continue to climb steadily, creating uncertainty in the market, but cryptocurrencies appear to be resilient in the face of these fluctuations. Polymarket's expansion into the U.S. market indicates a broader trend of increasing adoption and integration of blockchain technologies and prediction markets. This strategic move aims to capitalize on the growing demand for decentralized platforms and alternative investment opportunities. Overall, the crypto market is showing signs of strength and potential for further growth, with key players indicating positive sentiment and bullish outlooks.

data.title: Mercer International Inc. Updates Date for First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
data.description: On April 29, 2026, Mercer International Inc. (Nasdaq: MERC) stated a rescheduled release for its quarter ended March 31, 2026 financial results, initially planned for April 30, 2026, along with the related conference call. The company intends to announce its financial outcomes for the quarter concluded on March 31, 2026, on May 7, 2026, post market closure. The conference call has been pushed to May 8, 2026, at 10:00 AM ET.

data.title: Cizzle Brands Corporation Announces Major Canadian Retail Expansion with Launch of CWENCH Hydration™ at Walmart Canada and Expanded Distribution Across Loblaws and Real Canadian Superstores
data.description: A sports nutrition company called Cizzle Brands Corporation has disclosed its plans for expanding the retail distribution of their main brand, CWENCH Hydration™, across Canada. The brand will now be available at 197 Walmart Canada locations, marking a significant achievement for the company. Cizzle Brands is listed on Cboe Canada as CZZL, on OTCQB as CZZLF, and on Frankfurt as 8YF. This move demonstrates a major milestone for the company in broadening its consumer reach in the Canadian market.

data.title: UMC Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
data.description: In Taipei, Taiwan, United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC), a prominent semiconductor foundry, disclosed its first quarter of 2026 financial results. The consolidated revenue for the period stood at NT$61.04 billion, marking a 1.2% decline from the previous quarter's NT$61.81 billion. However, this revenue marked a 5.5% increase compared to the same period last year. The consolidated gross margin for the quarter was reported at 29.2%. The net income attributable to the shareholders of the company was not specified in the provided information.

data.title: South Korean companies Dunamu, Hana Financial, & POSCO start live blockchain remittance system.
data.description: Three major companies in South Korea, Hana Financial, POSCO International, and the operator of Upbit, Dunamu, have successfully implemented their blockchain remittance system into live trade transactions. This move signifies a significant step forward in utilizing blockchain technology for financial transactions within the country.

data.title: Polymarket is in discussions with the CFTC to expand US access post-2022 settlement, after a limited Dec 2025 US sports launch.
data.description: Polymarket is in discussions with the CFTC to reinstate American access after its legal agreement in 2022, subsequent to a targeted launch in the United States in December 2025 concentrating on sports contracts.
